title: ServerNotification
description:
Protocol-level notifications visible to all consumers.

69 variants across 21 categories. See `event-system-design.md` Section 2 and `engine-tui-unified-transcript-plan.md` ┬º4.1 for the history lifecycle category. Each variant's wire method is generated together with the matching `NotificationMethod` discriminant.

oneOf.description: Session result (final usage, cost, stop reason).
One Message appended to engine MessageHistory.

MessageHistory truncated to `keep_count` entries (indices
>= keep_count discarded). Emitted by explicit-rewind and
auto-restore both, so SDK + TUI converge on engine truncation
without separate private paths.

Session reset for resume. TUI clears derived transcript view
in preparation for a burst of `MessageAppended` that replays
the loaded JSONL transcript.

Bulk snapshot for resume hydration. Consumers replace the
derived transcript view wholesale (one cache-rebuild pass)
instead of processing N `MessageAppended` events sequentially.
Used when loading large JSONL transcripts where the
per-message channel-bounded path would stall at the 256-msg
queue boundary and force the engine task to yield. Live
appends still use `MessageAppended` ÔÇö this variant models
bulk replacement (a genuinely different operation).
Thread item updated (e.g. tool execution began).

LSP server pool finished prewarm. Fired once per session
bootstrap (after `LspManagerAdapter::prewarm` completes), so the
TUI status bar can show a `LSP` badge with the running-server
count. Not emitted when `Feature::Lsp` is off ÔÇö `started` /
`failed` are empty in that case.

Compaction phase progress (TS `onCompactProgress`).
Drives the spinner text in the TUI / SDK runner so the user
can see which sub-phase is active (PreCompact hooks ÔåÆ summarize
ÔåÆ PostCompact hooks ÔåÆ done).

Durable plan-item / V1 todo snapshot ÔÇö emitted after
`TaskCreate`/`TaskUpdate`/`TodoWrite` tools mutate state so
the TUI can refresh its panel without pulling the store
directly. TS parity: `notifyTasksUpdated` subscriber callback
in `utils/tasks.ts`.

Team lead received a plan-approval request from a teammate
(via mailbox). The TUI surfaces this as a modal overlay.
TS parity: `ExitPlanModeV2Tool.ts:137-141` teammate request flow.

A role's binding (model + provider + effort) changed in-memory
via the picker or `Ctrl+T`. Carries the resolved fields the TUI
needs to refresh its `model_by_role` cache and, for `Main`,
status-bar fields (`model`, `provider`, `thinking_effort`).
Hook execution progress (TS gap P1 ÔÇö stdout/stderr streaming).

Output from a user-executed local command (REPL `!` prefix).
Matches TS `SDKLocalCommandOutputMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1590-1602).

Files persisted to disk (file upload/snapshot completion).
Matches TS `SDKFilesPersistedEvent` (coreSchemas.ts:1672-1692).

MCP elicitation completed (form submission or cancellation).
Matches TS `SDKElicitationCompleteMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1779-1792).

Tool use summary from background haiku summarization.
Matches TS `SDKToolUseSummaryMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1769-1777).

Tool execution progress (bash/powershell long-running).
Matches TS `SDKToolProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1648-1659).
Sent at most once per 30 seconds per `parent_tool_use_id`.

Plugin state changed on disk (manifest added/removed/edited,
`installed_plugins.json` updated, or settings.json scope toggled).
Carries a short reason string the UI can surface as a banner.
TS parity: `useManagePlugins.ts:293-300` adds the "Plugins
changed. Run /reload-plugins to activate." notification. Never
triggers an auto-reload ÔÇö the explicit `/reload-plugins`
invocation is what applies the change.

AlreadyReadFilePayload.description:
TS parity: `AlreadyReadFileAttachment` (`utils/attachments.ts:323-333`).

TS carries the (potentially truncated) file content inline for UI display
even though `normalizeAttachmentForAPI` returns `[]`. coco-rs follows
suit ÔÇö `content` is the last-known file body used by transcript viewers.

content.description: Cached content from `FileReadState` at dedup time.

display_path.description: Path relative to CWD at creation time, for stable display.

truncated.description: Whether the content was truncated due to size limits.

ApiError.description: API error attached to an assistant message.

oneOf.description: Reasoning file content (file data that is part of reasoning).
Tool result (when assistant receives tool results).
Pre-rendered `LlmMessage` ÔÇö reaches the model when filtered in.
Typed silent payload ÔÇö UI/transcript only, never sent to the API.

AttachmentKind.description:
Every TS `Attachment.type` discriminator. 60 variants.

Wire format is snake_case via `#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]`
to match TS `Attachment.type` exactly, so transcripts round-trip.

oneOf.description:
TS: user-supplied per-turn critical instruction
(`toolUseContext.criticalSystemReminder_EXPERIMENTAL`).

**coco-rs dual role**: also serves as the generic carrier kind for
`coco_messages::create_meta_message` / `create_system_reminder_message`
(post-Phase-2 drop-in for the old `User{is_meta:true}` shape). The
reminder-generator path reads `ctx.config.critical_instruction`
for the TS-aligned case; engine-internal meta injection reuses the
same kind because API-visible + UI-hidden wrapped text is exactly
its visibility profile. Both paths land in `Message::Attachment`
with `AttachmentBody::Api(LlmMessage)`.

AttachmentMessage.description:
Attachment message: `kind` carries the TS-parity discriminant (60 variants),
`body` carries the typed payload.

**Invariant**: `kind` and `body` must agree ÔÇö e.g. `kind = HookCancelled`
must come with `body = Silent(SilentPayload::HookCancelled(..))`. Do **not**
construct via struct literal; use the typed constructor helpers below.

CancelReason.description:
Why a turn was interrupted. Lets the TUI distinguish "user pressed
Ctrl+C ÔÇö restore the input if conditions match" from "system cancelled
the in-flight turn to make room for `/clear` / `/compact` / `/rewind`
/ shutdown / next submit ÔÇö leave the conversation alone".

Mirrors TS `abortController.signal.reason` discrimination at
`REPL.tsx:3001`. New variants default to non-user-initiated semantics
at the consumer (no auto-restore), so adding e.g. `BudgetExhausted`
or `Timeout` later is additive without breaking the TUI handler.

oneOf.description:
User-initiated cancel (Ctrl+C in the TUI, `control/interrupt`
in the SDK). The only reason that may trigger auto-restore.

System pre-empted the in-flight turn so another session-level
operation can run (Clear / Compact / Rewind / Shutdown / new
SubmitInput). Auto-restore is suppressed ÔÇö the user did not
request a rewind.

CompactTrigger.description:
How compaction was triggered.

Stays in `coco-types` (rather than `coco-messages`) because
`event::CompactionPhaseParams` references it; the rest of the message
family lives in `coco-messages`.

Mirrors the TS taxonomy: manual `/compact`, threshold-based auto, PTL-413
reactive recovery, gap-based time-based microcompact, session-memory
short-circuit (no LLM), and staged context-collapse commit.

CompactionPhase.description:
Sub-phase of a compaction in progress (TS `onCompactProgress`).

Mirrors the TS phase taxonomy at Tool.ts:150-156:
  - `HooksStart { hook_type }` for PreCompact / PostCompact / SessionStart
  - `Summarizing` for the LLM summarizer call
  - `Done` to clear the spinner

hook_type.description: Set when `phase == HooksStart`. None when not applicable.

post_tokens.description: Estimated tokens after compaction (resulting context size).

pre_tokens.description:
Estimated tokens before compaction (post-strategy LLM input view).
`None` for paths that do not measure (e.g. micro/time-based may
skip when the savings are 0).

trigger.description:
Which strategy produced this compaction.

Mirrors the TS `tengu_compact.trigger` field. Older transcripts
without this field default to `Auto`. Defaulted via serde so
off-the-wire payloads from older SDK clients keep parsing.

CustomPart.description:
A custom content part for provider-specific extensions.

Used in both prompts (with `provider_options`) and responses (with `provider_metadata`).

EditedImageFilePayload.description:
TS parity: `edited_image_file` (`utils/attachments.ts:456-460`).

Image bytes can't be diffed textually; the UI renders a marker / thumbnail.

display_path.description: Path relative to CWD at creation time.

ElicitationCompleteParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKElicitationCompleteMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1779-1792).

Emitted after an MCP server's elicitation request is resolved
(either submitted or cancelled).

ExpandedView.description:
Which panel the TUI should have expanded in the task area.
TS parity: `AppState.expandedView` in `AppStateStore.ts` (3 variants:
`'none' | 'tasks' | 'teammates'`).

FilePart.description:
A file content part (image, document, etc.).

`data` is a tagged discriminated union:
- `Data { data }` ÔÇö raw bytes or base64-encoded string.
- `Url { url }` ÔÇö a URL pointing to the file.
- `Reference { reference }` ÔÇö a provider reference (`{ [provider]: id }`).
- `Text { text }` ÔÇö inline text content.

data.description: The file data as a tagged discriminated union.

mediaType.description:
Either a full IANA media type (`type/subtype`) or just the top-level
segment (e.g. `image`, `audio`).

FilesPersistedParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKFilesPersistedEvent` (coreSchemas.ts:1672-1692).

TS emits this when files are uploaded or persisted (e.g. after a
successful `filesApi` operation).

HookEventType.description:
27 hook event types matching TS `HOOK_EVENTS`
(`src/entrypoints/sdk/coreSchemas.ts:355-383`).

Wire format is **PascalCase** (e.g. `"PreToolUse"`) ÔÇö identical to
TS settings.json keys. Variant names serialize as-is via serde
default and strum default; do not add `rename_all`.

`#[non_exhaustive]` so future TS additions can land without
breaking match exhaustiveness in downstream crates.

InputTokenDetails.description:
Input-side token breakdown ÔÇö mirrors `vercel_ai::InputTokenDetails`
(which itself flattens `vercel_ai_provider::InputTokens`).

cache_read_tokens.description:
Input tokens served from the prompt cache. Both OpenAI-compat
(`prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`) and Anthropic
(`cache_read_input_tokens`) expose this.

cache_write_tokens.description:
Input tokens written to the prompt cache. **Only** the
Anthropic wire shape exposes this (`cache_creation_input_tokens`);
OpenAI-compat responses (DeepSeek, xAI, Groq, ÔÇª) have no
equivalent field and the value stays 0 there. Matches upstream
`@ai-sdk/openai-compatible`.

no_cache_tokens.description:
Input tokens that did NOT come from the prompt cache.
`input_tokens = no_cache_tokens + cache_read_tokens` when the
provider reports both.

LanguageModelV4FileData.description:
Generated file data ÔÇö either raw bytes/base64 or a URL.

Matches the 2-arm `SharedV4FileDataData | SharedV4FileDataUrl` tagged union
from the TypeScript v4 spec.

oneOf.description: A URL that points to the file.

LanguageModelV4Message.description: A message in a language model prompt.

LocalCommandOutputParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKLocalCommandOutputMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1590-1602).

TS emits this when the user runs a local bash command via the REPL `!`
prefix (not a tool call). The `content` field is the command output;
TS types it as the raw output structure (typically stdout/stderr).

root.description: Workspace root the prewarm anchored at.

started.description:
IDs of language servers that successfully spawned (e.g.
`["rust-analyzer", "gopls"]`).

McpConnectionStatus.description:
MCP server connection state on the wire.

Matches the TS `McpServerStatusSchema` enum at `coreSchemas.ts:167-173`:
`'connected' | 'failed' | 'needs-auth' | 'pending' | 'disabled'`.
`Disconnected` is a coco-rs extension used when the connection manager
has no record of a named server.

oneOf.description: coco-rs extension: server name unknown to the connection manager.

McpServerInit.description: MCP server init entry (inline struct in TS).

Message.description:
Top-level message enum.

Tool-use summaries are intentionally **not** a `Message` variant ÔÇö
they're UI-only polish (mobile-row label generated by the Fast
model post-turn). The engine emits
[`crate::ServerNotification::ToolUseSummary`] for SDK consumers
and the TUI side-caches the text without writing it to
`MessageHistory`. Keeping UI-only data out of the authoritative
transcript upholds I-3 from
`docs/coco-rs/engine-tui-unified-transcript-plan.md`.

ModelRole.description: Which purpose a model serves. Multiple roles can map to different models.

oneOf.description:
Plan-mode model. Used in two distinct contexts:

1. **Subagent role** ÔÇö when the built-in Plan subagent spawns
   (`SubagentType::Plan ÔåÆ ModelRole::Plan` in
   `core/subagent/src/subagent_role.rs:34`), the spawn factory
   resolves the client via `client_for_role(Plan)`.
2. **Main-session plan-mode swap** ÔÇö when the leader enters
   `PermissionMode::Plan`, the engine swaps the active client
   to `client_for_role(Plan)` for the duration of plan mode.
   TS parity behavioral analogue: `getRuntimeMainLoopModel`'s
   `opusplan` ÔåÆ Opus alias swap (`utils/model/model.ts:145-167`).
   coco-rs encodes this as a generic role slot so it works for
   any provider, not just Anthropic.

Unconfigured `models.plan` falls back to Main's spec via the
chain in `runtime.rs:507`, and `client_for_role` short-circuits
to the cached Main `Arc` ÔÇö both call sites degrade cleanly to
"no swap" without spurious cache breaks.

Forked-agent spawn (AgentTool / SkillTool). Distinct from
`Explore` ÔÇö Explore is an investigative subagent type that
happens to often be a "small fast" model; Subagent is the
generic spawn role used by tools/Agent and the swarm runtime.

ModelRoleChangedParams.description:
Payload for [`crate::ServerNotification::ModelRoleChanged`]. Carries
the resolved binding (model + provider + thinking effort) that the
TUI applies to `state.session.model_by_role[role]` and, when
`role == Main`, also to `state.session.{model, provider,
thinking_effort}` for the status bar.

Emitted by `tui_runner` after applying an in-memory override via
`SessionRuntime::apply_role_override` / `apply_role_effort`. No
persistence to settings.json ÔÇö that's the user's job.

effort.description:
`None` ÔçÆ engine falls back to the model's
`default_thinking_level`. `Some(_)` ÔçÆ explicit user choice.

reasoning_tokens.description:
Output tokens spent on reasoning / thinking blocks. Already
counted inside `output_tokens` and billed at the output rate.
Sourced from OpenAI-compat's `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens`
or Anthropic's thinking-block usage where exposed.

text_tokens.description:
Output tokens spent on plain text (non-reasoning).
`output_tokens = text_tokens + reasoning_tokens` when the
provider reports both.

PermissionMode.description:
Permission mode (top-level because it's used by both message and permission modules).

Wire format is camelCase to match TS `PermissionModeSchema` at
`coreSchemas.ts:337-347`: `z.enum(['default', 'acceptEdits',
'bypassPermissions', 'plan', 'dontAsk'])`. The serde aliases on the
drifting variants accept legacy snake_case input so old session
transcripts deserialize cleanly.

PlanApprovalRequestedParams.description:
A teammate's plan-approval request, surfaced to the team lead's
TUI for approve/deny. Payload byte-matches TS
`PlanApprovalRequestSchema` ÔÇö see `tools/ExitPlanModeTool/`.

plan_content.description: Plan text rendered from the teammate's plan file.

request_id.description: Correlation id carried back in the response envelope.

PluginInit.description: Plugin init entry (inline struct in TS).

PreservedSegment.description: Preserved message segment after partial compaction.

ProviderMetadata.description:
Provider-specific metadata attached to responses or stream events.

Similar to ProviderOptions but used for data returned from providers.

ProviderOptions.description:
Provider-specific options that can be passed to various API calls.

This is a map of provider names to their specific options.
For example: `{ "anthropic": { "thinking": { "type": "enabled" } } }`

ReasoningEffort.description:
Reasoning effort. Ordered from "off" through numeric intensity.
Provider-agnostic ÔÇö `thinking_convert` maps to per-provider wire shapes.

oneOf.description:
Explicit "thinking off". Convert layer skips typed-effort
emission; `level.options` may still carry an explicit-off
wire toggle (e.g. DeepSeek's `{"thinking":{"type":"disabled"}}`).

ReasoningFilePart.description:
A reasoning file content part (file data that is part of reasoning).

`data` is a 2-arm tagged union:
- `Data { data }` ÔÇö raw bytes or base64-encoded string.
- `Url { url }` ÔÇö a URL pointing to the file.

data.description: The file data (raw bytes/base64 or URL).

mediaType.description:
Either a full IANA media type (`type/subtype`) or just the top-level
segment (e.g. `image`, `audio`).

ReasoningPart.description: A reasoning content part (for thinking models).

SessionResultParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKResultMessageSchema` (coreSchemas.ts:1407-1451).
TS has two subtype variants (success/error) unified here with `is_error` flag.

errors.description: Error variant: list of error strings.

num_api_calls.description: coco-rs extension: num_api_calls for observability (not in TS).

permission_denials.description: Permission denials accumulated during the session.

result.description: Success variant: the agent's final result text.

SessionStartedParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKSystemMessageSchema` with subtype 'init' (coreSchemas.ts:1457-1494).
Sent once at session startup; carries the full bootstrap context the SDK
consumer needs to render a UI.

agents.description: Agent type names available for Agent tool spawning.

lsp_active.description:
`true` when at least one LSP server is healthy at session
startup (`Feature::Lsp` on + adapter `is_connected() = true`).
Used by the TUI status bar to render an "LSP" badge. Default
`false` keeps legacy SDK clients backward-compatible.

slash_commands.description: Slash commands available in this session.

version.description: Release version of the coco-rs binary.

oneOf.description: Turn completed, waiting for user input.
Waiting for user action (approval, question, elicitation).

SharedV4FileData.description:
File data as a tagged discriminated union (v4 spec).

- `Data` ÔÇö raw bytes or base64-encoded string.
- `Url`  ÔÇö a URL pointing to the file.
- `Reference` ÔÇö a provider reference (`{ [provider]: id }`).
- `Text` ÔÇö inline text content.

oneOf.description: A URL that points to the file.
A provider reference mapping provider names to file IDs.

reference.description: Map of provider name ÔåÆ provider-specific file ID.

oneOf.description: Inline text content (e.g. an inline text document).

SilentPayload.description:
Typed payload for silent attachment kinds.

Variant names map 1:1 to the [`AttachmentKind`](crate::AttachmentKind)
silent variants. Adding a new silent kind requires adding a matching
variant here ÔÇö enforced by the constructor helpers on
[`AttachmentMessage`](super::AttachmentMessage) +
the `silent_kind_round_trips_through_payload` parity test.

SourcePart.description: A source reference content part (for citations).

messages_summarized.description: Number of messages that were summarized.

pre_compact_discovered_tools.description: Tools discovered before compaction (for delta re-announcement).

SystemMemorySavedMessage.description:
Surfaced to the user's transcript when the auto-memory subsystem
has just landed new memory writes. TS parity:
`utils/messages.ts::createMemorySavedMessage(writtenPaths)` +
`services/autoDream/autoDream.ts:244-247` (which overrides
`verb` to `"Improved"` for dream consolidations vs. the default
`"Saved"` for extract).

verb.description:
Display verb. `"Saved"` for extract; `"Improved"` for auto-dream
consolidation. `#[serde(default)]` accepts older transcripts
produced before this field was added.

written_paths.description:
Paths the subagent wrote (or improved). TS `writtenPaths`. Excludes
`MEMORY.md` ÔÇö the index file is mechanical and not user-relevant.

SystemMessage.description:
System messages have sub-types for different notification kinds.
All system messages are `role: "user"` with `is_meta: true` for the API.

oneOf.description:
User cancellation marker. Replaces the legacy text-based
`INTERRUPT_MESSAGE` / `INTERRUPT_MESSAGE_FOR_TOOL_USE` User
messages with a typed variant. `for_tool_use` is computed once
by the engine cancel finalizer and never recomputed downstream;
see `engine-tui-unified-transcript-plan.md` ┬º7.1.

SystemMicrocompactBoundaryMessage.description:
Micro-compaction boundary marker.

Reserved for future use. coco-rs aligns with TS: micro-compaction
(client-side tool-result clearing + time-based MC) does NOT emit a
user-visible boundary message ÔÇö it broadcasts a `ContextCompacted`
event with `trigger=TimeBased`/`Auto` instead, and the TUI renders
a transient toast (`toast.micro_compaction`) rather than persisting
a boundary in the transcript. The variant stays defined so wire
transcripts that include one (e.g. cached-microcompact builds) can
be parsed without errors.

SystemUserInterruptionMessage.description:
Authoritative cancel marker. `for_tool_use` is computed by the engine
cancel finalizer (which sees the in-flight tool-call state) and stored
here; every other consumer reads the field. This eliminates the
engine Ôåö TUI recomputation race that the legacy text-based interrupt
markers exhibited.

TaskCompletedParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKTaskNotificationMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1694-1713).
TS calls this `task/notification`; coco-rs uses `task/completed` as the
wire method for brevity, but fields match TS exactly.

TaskListStatus.description:
Task status wire format ÔÇö matches TS `TaskStatusSchema`
(`utils/tasks.ts:69-74`). **Distinct** from [`crate::TaskStatus`],
which is the 6-variant running-task lifecycle enum.

TaskPanelChangedParams.description:
Snapshot of the task panel state ÔÇö tools emit this post-mutation
so the TUI can redraw without reaching into `ToolAppState` directly.

TaskProgressParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKTaskProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1750-1767).
In TS, `description` and `usage` are required; other fields optional.
The `workflow_progress` field carries the streaming state of local_workflow
tasks ÔÇö a delta batch of workflow state changes.

TaskStartedParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKTaskStartedMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1715-1733).
TS has `description` required and `task_type` optional.

ThreadItem.description:
Semantic representation of a conversation thread item.
Produced by `StreamAccumulator` from `AgentStreamEvent` sequences.
Used in `ServerNotification::ItemStarted / ItemUpdated / ItemCompleted`.

See `event-system-design.md` Section 1.6.

ThreadItemDetails.description:
Tool-specific semantic mapping.

Mapping rules (from `event-system-design.md` Section 6.2):
- Bash ÔåÆ `CommandExecution`
- Edit/Write ÔåÆ `FileChange`
- WebSearch ÔåÆ `WebSearch`
- mcp__* ÔåÆ `McpToolCall`
- Agent/Task ÔåÆ `Subagent`
- all others ÔåÆ `ToolCall`
- text content ÔåÆ `AgentMessage`
- thinking ÔåÆ `Reasoning`
- errors ÔåÆ `Error`

oneOf.description: Bash tool ÔåÆ command execution with output.
Edit/Write tools ÔåÆ file change with diff info.
All other tools (Read, Glob, Grep, etc.).

TodoRecord.description:
A TodoWrite item ÔÇö byte-matches TS `TodoItemSchema` (no `id` field,
positional identity).

TokenUsage.description:
Per-request token counts (returned by LLM API).

**Shape mirrors `vercel_ai::LanguageModelUsage`** ÔÇö the high-level
SDK type at `vercel-ai/ai/src/types/usage.rs`. Field names and
nesting are identical so a value can move across the seam without
information loss. `i64` is used in place of vercel-ai's `Option<u64>`
to match the rest of coco-rs's token-count idiom; "not reported"
surfaces as `0`.

Backward-compat: every nested field is `#[serde(default)]`, so old
session JSON without the breakdown fields parses cleanly with
breakdown values defaulting to 0.

input_tokens.description:
Total input tokens consumed (= `no_cache + cache_read + cache_write`
when all three are reported).

output_tokens.description:
Total output tokens consumed (= `text + reasoning` when both
are reported).

total_tokens.description:
`input_tokens + output_tokens`. Convenience aggregate matching
vercel-ai's `LanguageModelUsage.total_tokens`. Default to 0
when not provided; `Self::total()` recomputes if needed.

ToolApprovalRequestPart.description:
A tool approval request content part (for provider-executed tools).

This is used for flows where the provider executes the tool (e.g. MCP tools)
but requires an explicit user approval before continuing.

approvalId.description:
ID of the approval request. This ID is referenced by the subsequent
tool-approval-response (tool message) to approve or deny execution.

context.description: Additional context about the tool call (optional).

toolCallId.description: The tool call ID that this approval request is for.

ToolApprovalResponsePart.description:
A tool approval response part (for tools that require approval).

This contains the user's decision to approve or deny a provider-executed tool call.

approvalId.description: ID of the approval request that this response refers to.

approved.description: Whether the approval was granted (true) or denied (false).

reason.description: Optional reason for approval or denial.

providerExecuted.description:
Whether the tool call will be executed by the provider.
If this flag is not set or is false, the tool call will be executed by the client.

ToolProgressParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKToolProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1648-1659).

Long-running tool progress (Bash, PowerShell). TS throttles emission to
Ôëñ1 per 30 seconds per `parent_tool_use_id`. coco-rs StreamAccumulator
may emit this independently from `AgentStreamEvent::ToolUseStarted`.

ToolResultContent.description:
Content of a tool result.

This matches the LanguageModelV4ToolResultOutput type from the v4 spec.

oneOf.description: Text tool output that should be directly sent to the API.
Type when the user has denied the execution of the tool call.

reason.description: Optional reason for the execution denial.

ToolResultContentPart.description:
A part of tool result content.

Matches the `content` array items in `LanguageModelV4ToolResultOutput`
from the v4 spec ÔÇö TS source has 5 variants: `text`, `file-data`,
`file-url`, `file-reference`, `custom`. Image / non-image are
distinguished by `media_type` (image/png vs application/pdf etc.),
not by separate variants.

mediaType.description: IANA media type. TS spec carries this; Rust now honors it.

oneOf.description:
Provider reference (cross-provider file identifier mapping).
Replaces the old `FileId { file_id: ... }` variant; field is
`providerReference` to match the TS spec exactly.
Custom content part for provider-specific content.

isError.description: Whether the tool call was successful.

toolCallId.description: The tool call ID this result is for.

ToolUseSummaryParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKToolUseSummaryMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1769-1777).

Background Haiku-based summary of a batch of tool uses. TS uses this
to compress verbose tool output before it's displayed or archived.

reason.description:
Why the turn was interrupted. `None` only on legacy transcripts
that pre-date this field; new senders always populate it. The
TUI handler treats `None` as `SystemPreempt` (conservative ÔÇö no
auto-restore on unknown reason).

UnifiedFinishReason.description:
Unified finish reason for a completed LLM turn.

Multi-LLM-stable: each `vercel-ai-<provider>` adapter maps its raw
stop_reason into one of these variants. See module docs for the
per-provider mapping table.

oneOf.description:
Model finished a normal assistant turn (no tool calls, no
stop sequence match). Covers Anthropic `end_turn`/`pause_turn`,
OpenAI `stop` (when no stop_sequence matched), Google `STOP`
(no tool calls).

Stop sequence matched. Coco-rs refinement of the spec's
`Stop` bucket ÔÇö provider raw is `stop_sequence` /
`"stop-sequence"`.

Model invoked one or more tools and is awaiting results.
Anthropic `tool_use`, OpenAI `tool_calls`, Google `STOP +
has_tool_calls`.

Output-token budget exhausted. Anthropic `max_tokens`, OpenAI
`length`, Google `MAX_TOKENS`. Engine drives 64k escalate +
multi-turn recovery (`app/query/src/engine.rs`).

Context-window limit hit. Coco-rs refinement of the
`MaxTokens` bucket ÔÇö provider raw is
`model_context_window_exceeded`. Recovery path is shared with
`MaxTokens`; the variant exists so the user-facing wording
can distinguish "context window" vs "output token maximum".
Wire string keeps the Anthropic-original
`model_context_window_exceeded` form so transcripts that
captured the raw value round-trip via the typed enum.

Provider blocked / refused / safety-filtered the response.
Multi-LLM unified bucket ÔÇö Anthropic `refusal`, OpenAI
`content_filter`, Google `SAFETY` / `RECITATION` / `IMAGE_SAFETY`.

Provider reported an error during generation. Distinct from
network / HTTP / auth errors which surface via separate
error channels.

Unspecified / unknown termination. Raw wire string is
preserved on [`FinishReason::raw`] for diagnostics.

message.description: LLM API layer ÔÇö sent to API directly via .message field.

parent_tool_use_id.description:
Parent tool-use id when this message was produced by a tool
call's `new_messages` (e.g. inline skill expansion). Used by
transcript grouping + as a marker that the message is
transient until its parent tool_result resolves.
